claim: The Partition of 1947 and its accompanying mass violence — estimated 200,000 to 2 million dead, 10-20 million displaced — complicates a restrained-reordering reading of Gandhian independence: the transition was accompanied by one of the largest episodes of communal sacrifice violence in the twentieth century.
rationale: Coding the independence movement as restrained-reordering risks attributing Gandhi's anti-sacrificial philosophy to the outcome while suppressing the sacrificial violence that accompanied the actual transition; a more accurate coding might separate the independence movement from the Partition outcome.
